title: Removing certain Members from certain standing committees of the House of Representatives.
plain_summary: This bill removes four members of Congress from important committees they serve on. Mrs. Watson Coleman will no longer be on the committee that decides how the government spends money. Mr. Menendez will be taken off the committee that deals with energy and business issues. Mrs. McIver will be removed from two committees - one that handles homeland security and another that helps small businesses. The bill does not explain why these members are being removed from their committee jobs.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ethics.

sections.title: Resolution - Removing certain Members from certain standing committees of the House of Representatives
sections.text: This resolution removes specific House members from their committee assignments. Mrs. Watson Coleman is removed from the Committee on Appropriations. Mr. Menendez is removed from the Committee on Energy and Commerce. Mrs. McIver is removed from both the Committee on Homeland Security and the Committee on Small Business. These are permanent committees in the House of Representatives that handle different areas of government work and lawmaking.
